source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A piece of silicon is doped uniformly with phosphorous at a doping concentration of \( 10^{16} \, \text{cm}^{-3} \). The expected value of mobility versus doping concentration for silicon, assuming full dopant ionization, is shown in the <image>. The charge of an electron is \( 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \text{C} \). The conductivity (in \(\text{Sm}^{-1}\)) of the silicon sample at 300 K is:
extraction_notes: image field is present as a PIL image object placeholder, not a path or URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter B to choice text

image_dependency: question explicitly says mobility versus doping concentration is shown in the figure
the needed mobility value is not provided numerically in the text and must be read from the image
asset registry shows image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: requires extracting mobility from the figure at doping concentration 10^16 cm^-3
requires applying semiconductor conductivity relation using electron charge and fully ionized donor concentration
combines visual lookup with numerical calculation rather than a single direct read-off
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single scalar value
given full dopant ionization and electron charge, a solver can recompute conductivity once mobility is read from the figure
multiple-choice options provide an additional consistency check
image readability_score is reasonably high (0.8529), supporting downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present with no registry issues
image dependency is high and the required figure asset exists, so the core semantics are recoverable
multi-step and verifiability scores are strong, supporting useful downstream evaluation
graph-reading approximation introduces some noise, but it does not block solving or checking the item

agent_assessment.rationale: The problem clearly asks for silicon conductivity using the doping level, electron charge, and mobility read from the provided plot. The graph is legible enough to estimate the electron mobility near 10^16 cm^-3, and the multiple-choice answers make the expected numeric response clear. There is a minor possible unit-consistency issue, but the sample is still understandable.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is complete and jointly understandable, the image is clear enough for reading the needed mobility value, the rewritten open form is usable, and alignment/solvability evidence shows no serious failure. The minor unit-consistency risk is non-fatal.
